root_text.an3.80:1.1: Atha kho āyasmā ānando yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ nisīdi. Ekamantaṁ nisinno kho āyasmā ānando bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.an3.80:1.2: “sammukhā metaṁ, bhante, bhagavato sutaṁ sammukhā paṭiggahitaṁ:
root_text.an3.80:1.3: ‘bhagavato, ānanda, sikhissa abhibhū nāma sāvako brahmaloke ṭhito sahassilokadhātuṁ sarena viññāpesī’ti.
root_text.an3.80:1.4: Bhagavā pana, bhante, arahaṁ sammāsambuddho kīvatakaṁ pahoti sarena viññāpetun”ti?
root_text.an3.80:2.1: Dutiyampi kho āyasmā ānando bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.an3.80:2.2: “sammukhā metaṁ, bhante, bhagavato sutaṁ sammukhā paṭiggahitaṁ:
root_text.an3.80:2.3: ‘bhagavato, ānanda, sikhissa abhibhū nāma sāvako brahmaloke ṭhito sahassilokadhātuṁ sarena viññāpesī’ti.
root_text.an3.80:2.4: Bhagavā pana, bhante, arahaṁ sammāsambuddho kīvatakaṁ pahoti sarena viññāpetun”ti?
root_text.an3.80:3.1: Tatiyampi kho āyasmā ānando bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.an3.80:3.2: “sammukhā metaṁ, bhante, bhagavato sutaṁ sammukhā paṭiggahitaṁ:
root_text.an3.80:3.3: ‘bhagavato, ānanda, sikhissa abhibhū nāma sāvako brahmaloke ṭhito sahassilokadhātuṁ sarena viññāpesī’ti.
root_text.an3.80:3.4: Bhagavā pana, bhante, arahaṁ sammāsambuddho kīvatakaṁ pahoti sarena viññāpetun”ti?
root_text.an3.80:3.7: yaṁ bhagavā bhāseyya. Bhagavato sutvā bhikkhū dhāressantī”ti.
root_text.an3.80:3.9: “Evaṁ, bhante”ti kho āyasmā ānando bhagavato paccassosi.
root_text.an3.80:4.1: “Yāvatā, ānanda, candimasūriyā pariharanti, disā bhanti virocanā, tāva sahassadhā loko.
root_text.an3.80:4.2: Tasmiṁ sahassadhā loke sahassaṁ candānaṁ, sahassaṁ sūriyānaṁ, sahassaṁ sinerupabbatarājānaṁ, sahassaṁ jambudīpānaṁ, sahassaṁ aparagoyānānaṁ, sahassaṁ uttarakurūnaṁ, sahassaṁ pubbavidehānaṁ, cattāri mahāsamuddasahassāni, cattāri mahārājasahassāni, sahassaṁ cātumahārājikānaṁ, sahassaṁ tāvatiṁsānaṁ, sahassaṁ yāmānaṁ, sahassaṁ tusitānaṁ, sahassaṁ nimmānaratīnaṁ, sahassaṁ paranimmitavasavattīnaṁ, sahassaṁ brahmalokānaṁ—
root_text.an3.80:5.1: Yāvatānanda, sahassī cūḷanikā lokadhātu tāva sahassadhā loko.
root_text.an3.80:6.1: Yāvatānanda, dvisahassī majjhimikā lokadhātu tāva sahassadhā loko.
root_text.an3.80:7.1: Ākaṅkhamāno, ānanda, tathāgato tisahassimahāsahassilokadhātuṁ sarena viññāpeyya, yāvatā pana ākaṅkheyyā”ti.
root_text.an3.80:8.1: “Yathā kathaṁ pana, bhante, bhagavā tisahassimahāsahassilokadhātuṁ sarena viññāpeyya, yāvatā pana ākaṅkheyyā”ti?
root_text.an3.80:8.3: Yadā te sattā taṁ ālokaṁ sañjāneyyuṁ, atha tathāgato ghosaṁ kareyya saddamanussāveyya.
root_text.an3.80:8.4: Evaṁ kho, ānanda, tathāgato tisahassimahāsahassilokadhātuṁ sarena viññāpeyya, yāvatā pana ākaṅkheyyā”ti.
root_text.an3.80:9.1: Evaṁ vutte, āyasmā ānando āyasmantaṁ udāyiṁ etadavoca:
root_text.an3.80:9.4: Evaṁ vutte, āyasmā udāyī āyasmantaṁ ānandaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.an3.80:9.5: “kiṁ tuyhettha, āvuso ānanda, yadi te satthā evaṁmahiddhiko evaṁmahānubhāvo”ti?
root_text.an3.80:9.6: Evaṁ vutte, bhagavā āyasmantaṁ udāyiṁ etadavoca:
root_text.an3.80:9.8: Sace, udāyi, ānando avītarāgo kālaṁ kareyya, tena cittappasādena sattakkhattuṁ devesu devarajjaṁ kāreyya, sattakkhattuṁ imasmiṁyeva jambudīpe mahārajjaṁ kāreyya.
root_text.an3.80:9.9: Api ca, udāyi, ānando diṭṭheva dhamme parinibbāyissatī”ti.

translation_text.an3.80:1.1: Then Venerable Ānanda went up to the Buddha, bowed, sat down to one side, and said to him:
translation_text.an3.80:1.2: “Sir, I have heard and learned this in the presence of the Buddha:
translation_text.an3.80:1.3: ‘Ānanda, the Buddha Sikhī had a disciple called Abhibhū. Standing in the realm of divinity, he could make his voice heard throughout the thousandfold galaxy.’
translation_text.an3.80:1.4: I wonder how far a Blessed One, a perfected one, a fully awakened Buddha can make their voice heard?”
translation_text.an3.80:1.5: “He was a disciple, Ānanda. Realized Ones are immeasurable.”
translation_text.an3.80:3.1: For a third time, Ānanda said to the Buddha:
translation_text.an3.80:3.4: “… I wonder how far a Blessed One, a perfected one, a fully awakened Buddha can make their voice heard?”
translation_text.an3.80:3.5: “Ānanda, have you heard of a thousandfold lesser world system, a galaxy?”
translation_text.an3.80:3.6: “Now is the time, Blessed One! Now is the time, Holy One!
translation_text.an3.80:3.7: Let the Buddha speak. The mendicants will listen and remember it.”
translation_text.an3.80:3.8: “Well then, Ānanda, listen and apply your mind well, I will speak.”
translation_text.an3.80:4.1: “Ānanda, a galaxy extends a thousand times as far as the moon and sun revolve and the shining ones light up the quarters.
translation_text.an3.80:4.2: In that galaxy there are a thousand moons, a thousand suns, a thousand Sinerus king of mountains, a thousand Black Plum Tree Lands, a thousand lands west of Godāvarī, a thousand lands north of Kuru, a thousand lands east of Videha, four thousand oceans, four thousand great kings, a thousand realms of the gods of the four great kings, a thousand realms of the gods of the thirty-three, of the gods of Yama, of the joyful gods, of the gods who love to create, of the gods who control what is created by others, and a thousand realms of divinity.
translation_text.an3.80:4.3: This is called a thousandfold lesser world system, a ‘galaxy’.
translation_text.an3.80:5.1: A world system that extends for a thousand galaxies
translation_text.an3.80:5.2: is called a millionfold middling world system, a ‘galactic cluster’.
translation_text.an3.80:6.1: A world system that extends for a thousand galactic clusters
translation_text.an3.80:6.2: is called a billionfold great world system, a ‘galactic supercluster’.
translation_text.an3.80:7.1: If he wished, Ānanda, a Realized One could make his voice heard throughout a galactic supercluster, or as far as he wants.”
translation_text.an3.80:8.1: “But how would the Buddha make his voice heard so far?”
translation_text.an3.80:8.2: “First, Ānanda, a Realized One would fill the galactic supercluster with light.
translation_text.an3.80:8.3: When sentient beings saw the light, the Realized One would project his call so that they’d hear the sound.
translation_text.an3.80:8.4: That’s how a Realized One could make his voice heard throughout a galactic supercluster, or as far as he wants.”
translation_text.an3.80:9.1: When he said this, Venerable Ānanda said,
translation_text.an3.80:9.3: to have a teacher with such power and might!”
translation_text.an3.80:9.4: When he said this, Venerable Udāyī said to Venerable Ānanda,
translation_text.an3.80:9.5: “What is it to you, Reverend Ānanda, if your teacher has such power and might?”
translation_text.an3.80:9.6: When he said this, the Buddha said to Venerable Udāyī,
translation_text.an3.80:9.8: If Ānanda were to die while still not free of greed, he would rule as king of the gods for seven lifetimes, or as king of the Black Plum Tree Land for seven lifetimes, because of the confidence of his heart.
translation_text.an3.80:9.9: However, Ānanda will be fully extinguished in this very life.”

variant_text.an3.80:4.2: loke sahassaṁ → tasmiṁ sahassaṁ (sya-all, km, pts1ed)
variant_text.an3.80:7.1: tisahassimahāsahassilokadhātuṁ → tisahassiṁ mahāsahassiṁ lokadhātuṁ (sya-all, km); tisahassīmahāsahassī lokadhātuṁ (pts1ed)
variant_text.an3.80:9.1: āyasmantaṁ udāyiṁ → bhagavantaṁ (si ), cck, sya1ed, km potthakesu natthi. aṭṭhakathāya
